channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Enduring Influence of Carl Jung’s Psychological Theories
item.description: Carl Gustav Jung, a Swiss psychiatrist born in 1875, developed a distinct approach to psychology called analytical psychology, diverging from Freudian psychoanalysis. While Freud emphasised a personal...

item.title: BJP rejects claims of India’s involvement in US attack on Iranian warship IRIS Dena
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has strongly dismissed the Congress party’s claims that India bears any responsibility for the US attack on the Iranian naval vessel IRIS Dena. BJP leaders emphasised ...

item.title: Federal Information Minister to Address Fake Video Targeting Journalist Benazir Shah
item.description: Federal Information Minister Atta Tarar has publicly taken notice of a fake AI-generated video aimed at journalist Benazir Shah, calling the incident totally unacceptable and highly condemnable. The i...

item.title: Steve Carell stars in campus comedy 'Rooster' blending humour and family dynamics
item.description: Steve Carell returns to the small screen in 'Rooster', a 10-part comedy series co-created by Bill Lawrence of 'Ted Lasso' and Matt Tarses, co-creator of 'Scrubs'. Set on a university campus, the serie...

item.title: University of Ibadan Financial Report Shows High Spending on Honorarium and Refreshments Over Academic Needs in 2024
item.description: The audited financial statement of the University of Ibadan for the 2024 fiscal year shows serious spending on administrative perks. According to the report, the University spent N653 million on honor...

item.title: Kristi Noem continues to support Trump's policies moments after being dismissed from DHS
item.description: Kristi Noem, serving as the United States Secretary of Homeland Security, was abruptly dismissed by President Donald Trump during his second term. The firing was announced publicly while Noem was deli...

item.title: Gulf states face security and diplomatic challenges after US-Iran conflict escalates
item.description: The Gulf region is experiencing severe tensions following the US and Israel's bombing of Iran in retaliation for the killing of Iran’s supreme leader, Ali Khamenei. Iran responded with drone strikes t...

item.title: DILG directs attached agencies to implement fuel cost-cutting measures amid expected price surges
item.description: Due to anticipated further increases in fuel prices caused by the ongoing Middle East crisis, the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) has instructed its attached agencies to adopt s...

item.title: Google Faces Legal Challenge from Canadian Competition Authority
item.description: Google has encountered a legal setback in its ongoing dispute with Canada's competition regulator. The Competition Bureau of Canada has successfully challenged certain practices by Google that the wat...

item.title: NCACC Under Scrutiny for Authorising Arms Exports to Saudi Arabia and UAE Amid Yemen Conflict
item.description: The National Conventional Arms Control Committee (NCACC) of South Africa is facing intense scrutiny for its ongoing failure to provide transparency and accountability in arms export permits. Despite b...

item.title: James B Partridge's Primary School Bangers Brings Nostalgic Hymns to Adult Audiences
item.description: James B Partridge’s show, Primary School Bangers, has become a surprising cultural phenomenon in the UK, moving from viral TikTok videos to sold-out performances at arts centres and festivals, includi...

item.title: Why Plastic Recycling Struggles and How Change Can Happen
item.description: Despite the widespread use of recycling bins and public awareness, plastic recycling continues to fail globally. In Europe, only about 15% of plastics are recycled, while in the United States the figu...

item.title: Tanzania and TMRC launch micro-housing loans to help low-income citizens own homes
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The Tanzanian government has renewed its promise to work closely with financial institutions, including the Tanzania Mortgage Refinance Company (TMRC), to make credit facilities more a...

item.title: Britney Spears arrested on suspicion of DUI in California, released after overnight custody
item.description: Pop star Britney Spears was arrested by California highway patrol officers on Wednesday evening on suspicion of driving under the influence. She was detained at 9:28 PM and released the next morning a...

item.title: Why the Jim Carrey Clone Theory Gained Traction Online
item.description: Recently, a wave of online speculation claimed that <jim_carrey> had been replaced by a clone, following his appearance at the César awards in Paris. The theory arose simply because Carrey’s facial ap...

item.title: Sophie Oluwole: How She Reframed African Philosophy and Yoruba Thought
item.description: Sophie Oluwole (1935-2018) na one Nigerian scholar wey make history as the first woman wey get PhD for philosophy for Nigeria. She no only put Yoruba philosophical traditions for the world map, she al...

item.title: Federal High Court Orders Freezing of Petrocam Accounts Over ₦9.05bn Zenith Bank Debt
item.description: Federal High Court for Lagos don order say make dem freeze all the bank accounts wey connect to Petrocam Trading Nigeria Limited and im oga, Patrick Ilo, because of ₦9.05 billion wey Zenith Bank talk ...

item.title: Understanding Unsafe Abortion Practices Among Young Women in Ghana Despite Legal Access
item.description: Ghana law allow abortion for first trimester when pregnancy fit danger woman life, health, or if e happen due to rape or incest. Medication abortion, using mifepristone and misoprostol pills, dey avai...

item.title: Kganyago says South Africa’s economy is improving but warns key risks remain
item.description: South African Reserve Bank (SARB) Governor Lesetja Kganyago says South Africa’s macroeconomic environment has improved in recent years but cautions that policymakers and businesses should not become c...

item.title: President Approves Extension of NAB Chairman’s Term Amid Parliamentary Controversy
item.description: ISLAMABAD: President Asif Ali Zardari has formally approved the NAB (Amendment) Bill, 2026, which permits the extension of the National Accountability Bureau (NAB) chairman’s tenure by an additional t...
